        We will remediate the OpenSCAP finding sysctl_net_core_bpf_jit_harden by setting net.core.bpf_jit_harden to 2.

        Steps:

          1. Check the current runtime value of net.core.bpf_jit_harden. If it is not 2, set it to 2 using `sysctl -w net.core.bpf_jit_harden=2`.

          2. For each configuration file in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf (excluding any symlink to /etc/sysctl.conf), comment out any existing line that sets net.core.bpf_jit_harden (whether commented or not) by placing a '#' at the beginning of the line. This is to avoid duplicate configurations.

          3. Ensure that the file /etc/sysctl.d/bpf_jit_harden.conf contains the line "net.core.bpf_jit_harden = 2". If the file does not contain this line, append it. This makes the setting persistent across reboots.

        We will use the following commands:

              current=$(sysctl -n net.core.bpf_jit_harden); if [ "$current" -ne 2 ]; then sysctl -w net.core.bpf_jit_harden=2; fi

                if [ ! -L "$f" ] || [ "$(readlink -f "$f")" != "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]; then

              done

          Command 3:
              if ! grep -q '^net\.core\.bpf_jit_harden = 2$' /etc/sysctl.d/bpf_jit_harden.conf 2>/dev/null; then
                echo "net.core.bpf_jit_harden = 2" >> /etc/sysctl.d/bpf_jit_harden.conf
              fi
